commit_msg: feat(viewer): add dramatic time periods to timestamp display
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Clock Learned to Speak
subtitle: He gave time a voice, and it started telling on him.
when_where: 🕐 7:30 PM on main, the hour when the office lights flicker and nobody asks questions. His fingers were on the keyboard; the timestamp was on the screen. 🌃 The city outside his window had learned to measure itself in seconds. He was about to teach it something worse.
who_whom: 🔍 The viewer stared back at him—a simple display of time, now weaponized with *drama*. Every moment marked as "just now," "minutes ago," "hours past"—each label a small betrayal of how long he'd actually been sitting there. 💻 The app no longer just showed time. It *judged* it.
what_why: ⚙️ He wove dramatic periods into the timestamp display—a feature that should have been invisible, but instead became impossible to ignore. *Just now* screamed at him every time he refreshed. *Minutes ago* became an accusation. 🧠 The more granular the labels, the more his own time slipped through his fingers, each refresh a small confession that he was still here, still watching, still measuring the unmeasurable. 💀 He'd built a mirror that only showed him the present, and the present was all guilt.
verdict: He didn't add a feature; he added a prosecutor.
